definition_en: A person thought to be guilty of a crime
example: The police arrested a suspect in connection with the robbery.

definition_en: A person who sees a crime or accident happen
example: The witness gave a detailed description of the attacker.

definition_en: The decision made by a jury in a trial
example: The jury returned a not-guilty verdict.

definition_en: The punishment given by a judge after a conviction
example: He received a five-year prison sentence.

definition_en: Money paid to allow a suspect to be released before trial
example: He was released on bail pending further investigation.

definition_en: Information used in court to prove guilt or innocence
example: The detective collected evidence from the crime scene.

definition_en: To take someone into custody by legal authority
example: The officer arrested the man for shoplifting.

definition_en: The crime of illegally entering a building to steal
example: Several burglaries were reported in the neighbourhood.

definition_en: The crime of deceiving someone to gain money or advantage
example: He was charged with bank fraud.

definition_en: A formal examination of the facts of a crime
example: The police launched an investigation into the disappearance.

definition_en: An act that breaks a law
example: Drink-driving is a serious criminal offence.

definition_en: A lawyer who conducts the case against a defendant in court
example: The prosecutor presented strong evidence of guilt.

definition_en: To formally declare someone not guilty of a crime
example: The jury acquitted her of all charges.

definition_en: Evidence that someone was elsewhere when a crime was committed
example: He provided an alibi for the night of the robbery.

definition_en: The state of being under police control before or after a charge
example: The suspect was held in police custody overnight.

definition_en: The deliberate destruction or damage of property
example: Vandalism in the park cost the council thousands to repair.

definition_en: A person who attacks and robs someone in a public place
example: She was attacked by a mugger on her way home.

definition_en: Criminal activity carried out using the internet or computers
example: Cybercrime is one of the fastest-growing types of crime.

definition_en: Early release from prison under certain conditions
example: He was released on parole after serving half his sentence.

definition_en: A sum of money paid as a penalty for breaking a rule
example: She received a fine for parking illegally.

definition_en: Entering someone's property without permission
example: They were caught trespassing on private land.

definition_en: A police officer who investigates crimes
example: The detective interviewed all the witnesses.
